definitions.text: To incline, deviate, or bend, from a vertical position; to be in a position thus inclining or deviating.
examples: She leaned out of the window.

definitions.text: To incline in opinion or desire; to conform in conduct; often with to, toward, etc.
examples: I’m leaning towards voting Conservative in the next election.

definitions.text: To rest or rely, for support, comfort, to use as a hard surface for writing, etc.
An inclination away from the vertical.
examples: The trees had various leans toward gaps in the canopy.

definitions.text: Meat with no fat on it.
An organism that is lean in stature.
To thin out (a fuel-air mixture): to reduce the fuel flow into the mixture so that there is more air or oxygen.
A recreational drug composed of codeine-promethazine cough syrup mixed with usually soda and associated with the hip-hop culture of the Southern United States.
